description: A cricket team that has adopted a unique strategy in the IPL.

name: Ashish Nehra
description: Head coach of Gujarat Titans, known for his seam bowling expertise.
evidence_text: The design, perhaps, owed to their head coach, Ashish Nehra, a lethal exponent of seam bowling.

name: Kagiso Rabada
description: A key bowler for Gujarat Titans, known for his pace and bounce.
evidence_text: Rabada, of lithe limbs and graceful explosiveness, seams and skids the ball at high pace.

name: Mohammed Siraj
description: A bowler for Gujarat Titans, known for his swing and seam.
evidence_text: Siraj, exuding vigorous energy, invariably swings and seams the ball.

name: Jason Holder
description: A bowler for Gujarat Titans, known for his height and bounce.
evidence_text: Holder, lumbering to the crease, has one of the highest release points in the world.

name: Prasidh Krishna
description: A bowler for Gujarat Titans, known for his speed.
evidence_text: The similarly gangly Prasidh doesn’t purchase as much movement as Holder, but is quicker.

name: Rashid Khan
description: A leg-spinner for Gujarat Titans, known for his wicket-taking ability.
evidence_text: Holder is the middle-over enforcer, alongside leg-spinner Rashid Khan, who has stealthily picked up 16 wickets.

name: Sai Sudharsan
description: A batsman for Gujarat Titans, known for his ability to anchor innings.
evidence_text: Sudharsan has been dutifully playing that role.

name: Shubman Gill
description: A batsman for Gujarat Titans, known for his flexible batting style.
evidence_text: Gill could match Abhishek’s strike rate on a belter, but Abhishek cannot anchor like Gill or Sudharsan.

name: The Endocrine Society
description: A global network of people dealing with hormones.
evidence_text: The Endocrine Society, a global network of people dealing with hormones, announced that PCOS (Polycystic Ovarian Syndrome) would henceforth be known as PMOS (Polyendocrine Metabolic Ovarian Syndrome).

name: The Lancet
description: A medical journal that published an article discussing the name change.
evidence_text: As an article in The Lancet pointed out, the new name emerged out of a multistep global consensus process over the years.

name: PCOS Awareness Association
description: A non-profit organization focused on raising awareness and providing support for individuals affected by PCOS.
evidence_text: Our mission at the PCOS Awareness Association is to raise global awareness of Polycystic Ovarian Syndrome (PCOS).

name: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome
description: A disorder affecting women's hormonal and metabolic health, leading to infertility.
evidence_text: A disorder that affects 170 million women worldwide and is the leading cause of infertility, known as polycystic ovary syndrome, or PCOS.

name: Polyendocrine Metabolic Ovarian Syndrome
description: The new name for PCOS, chosen to better reflect the condition's impacts.
evidence_text: The new name, polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome, or PMOS, was chosen by a global coalition of patients, clinicians and medical organisations.

name: Androgen Excess and PCOS Society
description: An organization involved in the consensus to rename PCOS.
evidence_text: The consensus to rename the condition, led by several research groups and the Androgen Excess and PCOS Society.

name: Monash University
description: Led the global effort to rename PCOS.
evidence_text: In a landmark global effort led by Monash University

name: International Society of Endocrinology
description: Prof Helena Teede is the president.
evidence_text: president of the International Society of Endocrinology

name: Professor Helena Teede
description: Director of the Monash Centre for Health Research and Implementation.
evidence_text: said Professor Helena Teede, Director of the Monash Centre for Health Research and Implementation

name: Dr Madhuri Patil
description: President-elect of the Indian Society for Assisted Reproduction.
evidence_text: Dr Madhuri Patil, president-elect of the Indian Society for Assisted Reproduction

name: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS)
description: A common hormonal disorder affecting women, characterized by higher than normal androgen levels.
evidence_text: Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) is a common hormonal disorder in which higher than normal androgen levels lead to irregular menstrual periods, abnormal ovulation, infertility, excess facial or body hair and/or acne in women.

name: Endocrine Society
description: A global organisation of scientists and physicians devoted to hormone-related conditions.
evidence_text: The Endocrine Society, a global organisation of scientists and physicians devoted to hormone-related conditions...

name: Helena J. Teede
description: Author of the paper and director of Monash University’s Monash Centre for Health Research & Implementation.
evidence_text: Prof. Helena Teede, author of the paper, director of Monash University’s Monash Centre for Health Research & Implementation...

name: National Institute for Health Research (NIHR)
description: A research institute focused on non-communicable diseases.
evidence_text: The National Institute for Health Research (NIHR), Jodhpur, was established on 27 June 1984.

name: Desert Medicine Research Centre (DMRC)
description: The original name of NIHR when it was established.
evidence_text: established on 27 June 1984 as the Desert Medicine Research Centre (DMRC).

name: National Institute for Implementation Research on Non-Communicable Diseases (NIIRNCD)
description: The name NIHR was changed to this on 7 December 2019.
evidence_text: renamed the National Institute for Implementation Research on Non-Communicable Diseases (NIIRNCD) on 7 December 2019.

name: National Institutes of Health
description: The nation's medical research agency, which supported the study.
evidence_text: A large clinical trial supported by the National Institutes of Health (NIH) compared two commonly used treatments for pediatric patients treated for septic shock.

name: Pediatric Emergency Care Applied Research Network
description: A network for research in pediatric emergency medicine that led the study.
evidence_text: This research was supported by NIH grants R01HD101528 and P50DK114786 and led by the Pediatric Emergency Care Applied Research Network (PECARN).
